zotero.abstractNote: Deep neural networks are often trained with a cross-entropy loss, which can lead to feature collapse where features of the same class are mapped to the same point. To address this, we propose a hyperspherical uniformity loss that encourages features to be uniformly distributed on the hypersphere. We show that this loss can be interpreted as a regularization term that promotes feature diversity and improves generalization. We provide theoretical analysis and empirical evidence on various benchmarks to demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.
